# Customer Story Template

This template defines two output formats. The PM picks the format per run.

---

## Format A - Internal Customer Learnings (Default)

Derived from the team's standard customer story format (Global Motors, Apex Consulting, TelcoNet samples). This is the internal format used in strategy reviews and leadership presentations.

```markdown
# <Customer Name>

<1-2 sentence context paragraph. Include: who the customer is, scale of their environment (server count, datacenter count, regions), partnership context if relevant, and the migration or project scope. Ground every number in transcript evidence.>

## Issues & Pain Points

<Numbered list. Each issue gets a bold title line followed by 1-2 paragraphs of detail. Include: what went wrong, the impact on the customer's project, any workarounds they attempted, and scale context. Use the customer's own words where possible.>

1. **<Issue title - short, descriptive>**

   <Detail paragraph from transcript. Name specific tools, timelines, and team roles. Quantify impact where available.>

2. **<Issue title>**

   <Detail paragraph.>

3. **<Issue title>**

   <Detail paragraph.>

## Learnings

<Numbered list. Each learning gets a bold title followed by 1-2 paragraphs explaining what the engagement revealed. Include compete perspectives where relevant, marked with "Compete perspective:" prefix.>

1. **<Learning title - what did this engagement teach us?>**

   <Detail paragraph. Tie the learning to product gaps, strengths, or customer behavior patterns.>

   - **Compete perspective:** <How [Competitor 1]/[Competitor 2]/third-party handles the same scenario. Use factual comparisons, not marketing claims.>

2. **<Learning title>**

   <Detail paragraph.>

3. **<Learning title>**

   <Detail paragraph.>

## Action Items, Owners & ETAs

<Numbered list. Each action item includes a description, owner name, and target date/quarter.>

1. <Action description>. Owner: <Name>. ETA: <Date or Quarter>.

2. <Action description>. Owner: <Name>. ETA: <Date or Quarter>.

3. <Action description>. Owner: <Name>. ETA: <Date or Quarter>.

## Current Customer Status / Testimonial

<Bullet list of status updates and direct customer or field quotes. Attribute each quote to a speaker role (customer, field specialist, specialist, account team, partner).>

- <Status or quote with attribution>
- <Status or quote with attribution>
```

### Format A Checklist

- [ ] Customer context paragraph includes scale numbers (servers, apps, regions)
- [ ] Every issue title is specific (not generic like "Performance problems")
- [ ] Every issue paragraph names tools, timelines, or team roles from the transcript
- [ ] Learnings tie back to product gaps or strengths, not just observations
- [ ] Compete perspectives are factual comparisons, not marketing claims
- [ ] Action items have named owners and target dates
- [ ] Every quote is verbatim or near-verbatim from transcript with speaker attribution
- [ ] No metrics or numbers were invented - all sourced from transcript or PM input
- [ ] Humanizer check passed

---

## Format B - Public Case Study

For external publication on your company blog, your product blog, or partner channels. Tells the customer's story for a broader audience.

```markdown
# <Customer Name> <action verb> <outcome> with <your company Product>

## Customer Profile

| | |
|---|---|
| **Company** | <Name> |
| **Industry** | <Industry> |
| **Size** | <Employee count, revenue, or scale metric> |
| **Country/Region** | <Location> |
| **Products Used** | <your product, your other product, etc.> |

## The Challenge

<2-3 paragraphs describing the customer's situation before your company engagement. Lead with the business problem, not the technology. Include specific scale numbers and timelines. Use customer quotes to add authenticity.>

> "<Direct quote from customer about their challenge>"
> - <Name> | <Title> | <Company>

## The Solution

<2-3 paragraphs describing what your company and the customer did together. Name the specific products, features, and approaches used. Walk through the journey chronologically - what happened first, what followed, what the experience was like.>

> "<Direct quote from customer about working with your company or the product>"
> - <Name> | <Title> | <Company>

## The Results

<1-2 paragraphs summarizing outcomes, followed by a numbered list of key metrics.>

1. **<Metric>** - <context and business impact>
2. **<Metric>** - <context and business impact>
3. **<Metric>** - <context and business impact>

> "<Direct quote from customer about results or forward-looking statement>"
> - <Name> | <Title> | <Company>

## What's Next

<1 paragraph on the customer's forward-looking plans with your company. Keep it grounded in what was discussed in the transcript, not aspirational marketing.>
```

### Format B Checklist

- [ ] Title follows the pattern: Customer + action verb + outcome + with + product
- [ ] Customer profile table is complete (company, industry, size, region, products)
- [ ] Challenge section leads with business problem, not technology
- [ ] Solution section names specific products and features used
- [ ] Results section has at least 2 quantified metrics
- [ ] At least 2 direct customer quotes with full attribution (name, title, company)
- [ ] Every quote is verbatim or near-verbatim from transcript
- [ ] No metrics invented - all sourced from transcript or PM input
- [ ] "What's Next" is grounded in transcript discussion, not speculation
- [ ] Humanizer check passed
